sectionOrderRules: Hero must state what the company manufactures and include a primary contact CTA.
Product-summary must highlight concrete product families or material categories, not vague claims.
Trust-proof must use real business facts such as organisation details, references or clear process information.
If production-summary is present, it should appear before trust-proof.
Final CTA should invite contact for quote, specification dialogue or delivery questions without promising unavailable services.
Products-intro must explain the scope of the offering in plain language.
Product-category-list items must be concrete and describe product type, common use or ordering basis when known.
Do not present products as an ecommerce checkout if the business primarily sells via enquiry.
Pricing-overview may only show honest price examples, ranges or request-for-quote messaging when exact pricing is not available.
Production-intro must connect the manufacturing setup to customer needs such as series, dimensions, finish or delivery.
Materials-and-process should describe actual materials or process steps at a high level without inventing technical certifications.
Capacity-and-delivery should stay factual and avoid unsupported lead-time promises.
If quality-approach is shown, it should focus on controls, consistency, documentation or experience only when supported by input data.
Contact-info must include real phone, email and address details when available.
Contact-form should be framed for quote requests, product questions or delivery enquiries.
If pickup-or-delivery-info is shown, it should clarify practical logistics such as loading, collection or reception times.
Map should appear after core contact details unless the page is very short.
